Regent Symbol of Innoruuk  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 7
STR: +2 WIS: +3 MANA: +15
Effect: Clinging Darkness (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 15
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: CLR SHM
Race: HUM DEF TRL
Deity: Innoruuk
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Regent Symbol of Innoruuk
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: NECK
Charges: Unlimited
AC: 7
STR: +2 WIS: +3 MANA: +15
Effect: Clinging Darkness (Any Slot/Can Equip, Casting Time: 6.0) at Level 15
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: CLR SHM
Race: HUM DEF TRL
Deity: Innoruuk
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Lore:Smokey Quartz Unholy Symbol
Item Type:Armor
Light:Fire Beetle Eye
Stackable:No
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:15:30
Page Updated:Wed Mar 8th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 30
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Innoruuk Regent

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Neriak Third Gate
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Minimum Level
QuoteReply
# May 29 2022 at 10:43 AM Rating: Good
Xanfi
Scholar
39 posts
As of 2022 on Yelinak TLP there is a minimum level of 16 to complete the quest. At level 15 Ithvol told me to go kill more rats, so I tried skipping him, but then the Dragoon in LFay told me he had no need for it... then I went ahead and tried the gnome lady in Steamfont and she told me to kill rats etc.

Leveled to 16 (no other faction work) and was able to complete the entire quest no problem.

Also, as a fresh Troll Shaman, I had to do the faction quest 1 time. So before you start running to Neriak, go to East Freeport and say "I am devoted to Innoruuk" to Saxarivza Zaxun, and get the letter to deliver to Perrir Zexus in Neriak for faction.

Edit: just tried something with another troll shaman- if you have a Guise of the Deceiver (DE illusion mask), using that will take you from apprehensive faction to amiable, and you can do the quest turn ins with no pre-faction questing.

Edited, Jun 29th 2022 10:00am by Xanfi
▲ Page top
Still limited as listed in 2019, FWIW
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2019 at 5:26 PM Rating: Good
Vomitous
Scholar
45 posts
If you're like me and decided based on the 2012 comment to give the quests a run to see what its current status is(in 2019) - as with many other things, it hasn't actually changed and the correct info was here on Allakhazam. I got part way through the quest line on a Troll BST, but in the intermediate quest(Innoruuk Disciple) there is a gate on who can use the Halfling mask, namely CLR/SHM, HUM/DEF/TRL/FRG.
Looking at the final item, and the 'Can Equip' requirement for the clicky, we can say that it may be possible for an Innoruuk Froglok to complete the quest but be unable to use it, but everyone else outside of that list(Innoruuk or not) can't even get to the third quest.
▲ Page top
Cast from inventory
QuoteReply
# May 22 2012 at 2:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1632097665382
3 posts
I do not know when they changed it, but this item can now be cast from inventory. So I do not know if other classes can now use it if they have the faction to do the quest.
▲ Page top
Update the quest line PLEEZ
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2007 at 8:24 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592310956561
7 posts
This item rocks. Now lets see Sony update all these quests, I woudl like to see this go to a snare a step or two higher, I think Engulfing Darkness would not be too much to ask, with a casting time of about 4 or 5 sec, a bit better, but not overpowered for casting time.

As for teh beer summoning one, it shoudl summon an acohol so powerful it instantly kills you when you drink it. :)
▲ Page top
Re-roll as a Troll
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2007 at 3:19 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592773507307
7 posts
I did this quest recently and I really enjoyed it. I already had a young Vah Shir shammy but I was increasing frustrated by his inability to snare as the seasons (levels) passed and the unreliability of rooting. (Also a warrior once told me years ago that rooting causes mobs to hit harder but I cannot confirm) I saw some folks debating about the best shammy races in General Chat one day and they mentioned that Trolls had the ability to snare and I started asking questions. Well my shammy was still young enough to re-roll so I did. I also wanted the Workers Sledgemallet from OT or maybe I'm just evil? (muhahaha)

Anyways, I completed the quest at around 18 with the help of my 55 tank on the same account. (log & loot) I played EQ years ago and have just returned so I'm generally well versed about the game; I just don't have a high level t00n yet.

The required faction to start this quest can easily be acquired by one of the two ways mentioned below. Either turn in the letter in Grobb (I bound myself by High Priest Perrir Zexus in the 3rd Gate and used "Go to Home City" the next day) or grab a DE illusion in the Bazaar and do the wine turn in. (The wine can easily be purchased in the same zone)

There really isn't a bottleneck to the quest but I did wanna add some side notes. There is no trick to Woopal just a matter of clearing and waiting =p. Wallin Slyfoot was actually difficult for me to find even with a tracker. At some points he won't stop when hailed and I was only able to spot him when it was morning. Then I had a hell of a time finding the "spy" despite the LOC. Once you go up the waterfall just continue to walk straight ahead through a false wall and you drop down and aggro the spy.

During the finally few steps once you find Ennixy in FD you don't need faction to spawn Swiftmoon so have a higher level say "Hate be my guide." to her, locate him along the path from Ak'kanon, aggro him, drop him near where your baby shammy is logged, switch over and loot up.

I was formerly a necro years ago & I love this little trinket. Rooting is so unreliable when u need it most. You'll cast this during battle until it sticks and rest assured a train will not de-rail your tea party. Keep in mind it lasts for 8 ticks so keep root loaded as a back up obviously and it snares at 10% if you're wondering why the pathing distance seems to be random. You'll learn how to properly place your DoTs to control this distance as even a small hike by a dying NPC can spark a nasty train.
▲ Page top

QuoteReply
# Apr 06 2006 at 8:18 PM Rating: Default

6 posts
this quest is soloable in about 90 mins start to finish did this today with my 48 DE Inny Cleric had my 69 dru 2boxed for sow ect ... hardest part was getting that one dude to spawn always seemed his PH was up he took bout 45 mins to spawn but after that it was a cake walk



Mezzenufast 70/326 Enchanter
Shadowzfang 66/15 Warrior
Paynekiller 48 Cleric

Zaxun / Zexus
QuoteReply
# Oct 31 2005 at 2:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592260520447
Scholar
53 posts
Zaxun / Zexus which on is the right?

The answer to all of this is the following:
In Grobb at the shammy guild (-53, -433) there is 'Savarixsa Zexus' which give you the note to raise your Innoruk faction . Well the note is sigend by Zaxun but well - it works.

In Freeport East at the dark side Cleric GMs there is 'Savarixsa Zaxun'. I didn't manage to hail or even speak to her. (also tried to hail her as Zexus).

I reported this bug, let's see if they fix it.

i need the snare effects

# Jul 05 2004 at 8:13 AM Rating: Decent

Unfortunately no. Any item with "Must Equip." next to the effect can only be equipped and used by the listed class/race combinations.

Level or faction based?
QuoteReply
# Jan 10 2004 at 1:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261063947
3 posts
It there a restriction by level for the final part? Ithval keeps telling me "go hunt beetles instead" and "come back after you have killed some rats". I'm level 14 (supposedly it procs at that level), but he wont give me the quest. If i try to bypass it, I only get as far as the Dragoon, who takes the crate and tells me my hate is not strong enough.

How did people get this at level 9 if its level based or is that just a clever way of saying "get more faction"?

**** Update: I tried each level and 16 is the magic number, also carried that letter from Freeport a couple more times which helped faction some more *****

Edited, Sat Jan 10 23:56:36 2004
▲ Page top
Level or faction based?

16 posts

Anyone found the next step?
QuoteReply
# Dec 25 2003 at 8:09 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592260630515
5 posts
Hi all.
I have had this item for a long time now and was just wondering if anyone has found the next part/upgrade for this quest line....
The Qeynos Badge of Honor (the "good" version of this quest liine) is now up to i think necklace 6 or 7...increasing the necklaces stats but retaining the "See Invis" effect.

It would be nice to have better stats but still retain the snare effect...anyone found the next one??

THanks
Jamit
Shaman Rathe.
▲ Page top
RE: Anyone found the next step?
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2004 at 7:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591785966839
9 posts
The Badge of Honor quests are not a good version of this quest. The Honor ones are wearable by all/all and not diety restricted as this one is. The other diety restriced cleric quest items that I have seen all go to three steps and stop there. So this is probaby the end of that line of quests for clerics of Innoruk. By this level you could be workin on your Sol Ro armor, and depending on you play style and current equipment that may or may not be a good way to get some items you will use.
▲ Page top
Any similar thing
QuoteReply
# Aug 26 2003 at 8:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759745291
View User Forum
16 posts
for barb shammies available?
▲ Page top
how to raise faction...
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2003 at 5:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255583405
11 posts
go to the bazaar and buy 1 essence of dark elf potion

then buy about 20 stacks of red wine (local merchants sells them)

in 3rd gate go to the library and give Lokar To'biath 4 bottles of red wine ...he will reward you with exp and FACTION ....but only if you use the dark elf potion ....when you max out your faction ..change back into a troll by clickin the icon off and continue with the wine quest ....this sure beats killing 8-10 hours worth of halftlings...enjoy.

Edited, Fri Jun 13 18:07:51 2003
▲ Page top
RE: how to raise faction...
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2003 at 12:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787585825
2 posts
You can do this very quickly as a troll, without wine or dark elf illusions. Go to Freeport and find your way to the underground evil guilds. In one of the upper rooms of the underground guildmaster areas, you'll see a dark-elf cleric. Here's the catch. Her last name right now is Zaxun. (Her first name is an odd one.. Sax-something). You need to MANUALLY type in "Hail, (firstname) Zexus" and she will respond with what to do next to receive your faction. If you just use the H key, you will hail her by her wrong last name, and she will not respond.

Tricky.. I did this yesterday, so I know it works.
▲ Page top

▲ Page top
No moaning lighties...
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2002 at 10:16 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592254362167
20 posts
I have the piece like this for the lighties on my WE Druid (Tunarean Symbol of summut).

It's a manafree root, which is LOVELY for when you are fighting something and run low on mana, to root it while you med for that final nuke or dot, instead of having to kite or blow mana on a non-mana-free root. I can't tell you how many times the root neck piece has saved my druid (her life or a quad gone wrong), and I know other lighties who have said the same.

I can't wait to get this snare item for my inkie cleric, as my cleric's hunting partner is a BL, and they don't get snare either. This should make life much easier.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 19 2003 at 10:43 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) *Cough* Uh... Darkness is a snare, not a root. Pointless to comment on, but I just wanted to get the facts straight so if anyone needs dire info on this item they get the right info.
RE: No moaning lighties...

▲ Page top
Trolls
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2002 at 2:46 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765425335
16 posts
Ok, I went all the way back to Grobb to find out for myself who the note goes to. The note goes to Perrir, I believe it was in the neriak cleric guild, neriak 3rd gate. He is wearing full silver platemail and is easy to spot.

All trolls must do this part to be able to do the initiate quest. Handing in the note will raise your faction to amiably, from apprehensive right away. Alas, this is still not enough to do the quest. Next, perrir asks you to obtain leatherfoot raider skullcaps and bring them to him, to prove your worth to the priests of innoruuk.

What you need to do next is, kill leather foot deputies in nektulos forest, or the one that spawns in neriak commons, near the blind fish in the water. The ones in nektulos can be lvl 10-40. The one in the commons is usually about lvl 15-16, but he heardly ever drops the cap.

Once you hand in enough caps, you can do the quest. I had to turn in about 8 caps before I could do the quest. To check your faction, hail perrir, or ithvol'j kasn. If it is not high enough, he will say, I can see hate breeding in you, blah blah but you need to do more work. If he responds with a quest, wah lah, you can now start.

Edited, Tue Oct 1 14:59:33 2002
▲ Page top
Troll Faction
QuoteReply
# Sep 27 2002 at 4:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765425335
16 posts
I am a Troll shaman trying to do this quest. Ithvol won't talk to me. I need to raise my faction.

One guy said kill halflings, but I need to get the letter from Grobb first. Well I got the letter but who do I give it to?! I forgot who she told me to bring it to.

Also, killing halflings doesn't give me a faction hit that says, priests of innoruuk got better. So I really don't think it's raising my faction!

Could anyone please tell me how to raise my faction so I can do this quest, thanks.
▲ Page top
RE: Troll Faction
QuoteReply
# May 26 2003 at 11:16 AM Rating: Decent
Liam
Scholar
20 posts
Any time you do a quest where all you do is hail or speak to someone, just do it again if you forget what to do. The only thing it will do is say duplicate lore items not allowed but will tell you the original instructions again. At least that is what happens on most quests I have seen.
